title: Auth Callback Handling on the Central Auth Domain
description:
The auth domain must complete the Stytch authentication flow and securely hand off the authenticated session to the target tenant subdomain.

## Requirements

- Auth domain validates the Stytch response and creates an internal "handoff" artifact
- Return URL is validated against an allowlist to prevent open redirect vulnerabilities
- Tenant context is carried via a signed state parameter to prevent tampering
acceptance_criteria: Auth domain validates Stytch callback response before creating handoff artifact
Return URLs are validated against an allowlist of known tenant subdomains
Open redirect attacks via manipulated return URLs are blocked
Tenant context in the state parameter is signed and tamper-proof
Invalid or expired state parameters are rejected with a safe error page
middleware_changes: Auth callback handler on the auth domain: validates Stytch response, checks return URL against allowlist, verifies signed state parameter, and generates handoff artifact for the target subdomain.
implementation_notes: The handoff artifact (likely a short-lived OIDC authorization code) allows the target subdomain to establish a session without the auth domain sharing cookies. The signed state parameter prevents CSRF and ensures the callback matches the original login request.
